steps.title: Match the intellectual claim
steps.content: The claim says Newland compares his intellect favorably to that of other men in the audience. The supporting quotation must show Newland judging his own reading, thinking, or worldly experience as superior to theirs.

steps.title: Reject off-target quotations
steps.content: Choice B is about anticipation of pleasure. Choice C describes the opera setting. Choice D concerns social conformity and the men's collective influence, not Newland's intellectual self-comparison. The remaining quotation directly says he had read more, thought more, and seen more of the world. The answer is A.
